content.text: Circuit Tracks has two global effects: Delay and Reverb. Each track can send its audio to these effects in varying amounts.
Press the FX button to enter effects mode. The macro knobs now control the effects parameters, and the pads show send levels for each track.
The delay effect creates echoes of your sound. In FX mode, the left side of controls affects delay:

rows: Delay Time - Length of the delay
Delay Hi-Pass - Filter out low frequencies from delays
Delay Lo-Pass - Filter out high frequencies from delays

content.title: Tempo Sync
content.text: Delay time can sync to your project tempo. At certain values, it locks to musical divisions (1/4, 1/8, etc.).
Reverb adds space and ambience. In FX mode, the right side controls reverb:

rows: Reverb Decay - Length of the reverb tail
Reverb Hi-Pass - Remove low frequencies from reverb
Reverb Lo-Pass - Remove high frequencies from reverb

content.text: Each track has independent send amounts for both effects. In FX View, the macro knobs (1-8) control send levels for each of the 8 tracks. The pads let you toggle between Reverb and Delay send views.

items: Top pad row (Reverb): Select this row, then use Macro 1-8 to set reverb send for each track
Bottom pad row (Delay): Select this row, then use Macro 1-8 to set delay send for each track

content.text: Effect sends are saved per-pattern. This lets you have different effect amounts in different sections of your song.
While Circuit Tracks doesn't have true sidechain compression, you can achieve similar pumping effects by automating effect sends or filter cutoff in sync with your kick drum pattern.
